title: rosa × dumetorum var. acanthina
intro: Rosa × dumetorum var. acanthina, also known as the wild rose or prickly rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a variant of the Rosa × dumetorum hybrid, characterized by its thorny stems and fragrant, pink flowers. Rosa × dumetorum var. acanthina has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and wounds. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in the production of rose oil, which is valued for its calming and antiseptic effects.
